title: Dasystephana fetissowii
intro: Dasystephana fetissowii, also known as the 'Burmese rose', is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana fetissowii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
